title: Cargo Ejected by Type
query: SELECT SUM(Count) AS total FROM EjectCargo WHERE event_timestamp >= :startDate AND event_timestamp < :endDate
detailQuery: SELECT COALESCE(Type_Localised, Type) AS label, SUM(Count) AS value FROM EjectCargo WHERE event_timestamp >= :startDate AND event_timestamp < :endDate GROUP BY Type ORDER BY value DESC LIMIT 20

details.text: {total} unit(s) of cargo ejected during the report period. Jettisoned cargo is either a tactical sacrifice — dumping contraband before a police scan — or operational waste from an overfull hold. The types tell you whether you were running hot legally or disposing of something the authorities definitely would have noticed.
details.help: EjectCargo Type_Localised is the commodity type ejected. total drives the title as the jettison volume headline.

tagLines: Cargo jettison log compiled. Ejected commodity types noted in the flight record.
You threw it out. The void has it now. Most of the time there was a very good reason.
Jettisoning cargo is a decision made in seconds. The reasons for it vary enormously. The log just records the result.
